⍞ Materials: Across This Land

PREP: Sketch or print the following blank map for today.
∞ Map Link: Blank Map

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
~15 pages.
Copy Map P1 from page xvi from the Preface.

➜ MAPWORK
Create one map, adding interesting details from each reading as you go along.

If interested:
∞ Article Link: Idaho Batholith

⍞ Materials: Five Months on the Missouri River

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Chapter 1 Genesis of a Dream

Narrate silently, which means to go over in your mind what you read. You are narrating to yourself instead of in writing or orally. Start at the beginning and ask yourself what came next until you have gone over the chapter. It may seem strange at first, but you will quickly get used to it.

➜ WRITE
Compose 5 questions and/or 5 reflections about the chapter.
Create or sketch a map as you travel down the river. Add places or items of interest to your map.

If interested:
∞ Video Link: Carving the Canoe

⍞ Materials: Sufferings in Africa

➜ READ
Start on p.x1 "To the Reader" and read ~23 pages or what you can read in 30 minutes.

➜ MAPWORK
Look at the map of Africa that was included with the book when it was published in 1817. Compare the 1817 map to a current map. Familiarize yourself in general with where the events will take place.

∞ Book Link: Original manuscript of the book
∞ Image Link: Map included in the original book
∞ Map Link: Current map of the region

➜ NARRATE: Consider creating a journal entry from Captain Riley of the reading's events and sketch a simple map to go with it. Or, come up with another creative way to narrate the readings.
